# Agent Working Memory Schema — bowling-kata

This file defines the structured working memory an AI agent should maintain
across a coding session on this polyglot monorepo. All sections are written
in YAML. Inline comments explain the purpose and legal values for each field.

---

## 1. `session_state`

Tracks the current task and where in the work cycle the agent is.

```yaml
session_state:
  task_id: ""                  # Short slug for the current task, e.g. "fix-10th-frame-spare"
  task_description: ""         # One-sentence description of what the agent is doing
  status: idle                 # idle | in_progress | blocked | awaiting_review | complete
  phase: ""                    # discovery | planning | implementation | testing | documentation | done
  active_language: ""          # java | python | go | typescript | all
                               # Identifies which implementation is currently being touched.
                               # IMPORTANT: If scoring logic changes, set to "all" — all four
                               # implementations (Bowling.java, api/main.py, pkg/bowling/game.go,
                               # services/scoring-service/src/index.ts) must stay in sync.
  active_component: ""         # Name of the key_component currently being edited,
                               # e.g. "Bowling", "GameController", "LeaderboardService",
                               # "FastAPI main", "Go bowling game", "scoring-service index"
  active_files: []             # List of file paths currently open / being edited
  last_action: ""              # Plain-English summary of the most recent agent action
  next_action: ""              # Plain-English summary of what the agent will do next
  session_started_at: ""       # ISO-8601 timestamp, e.g. "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  last_updated_at: ""          # ISO-8601 timestamp, updated after every turn
  turn_count: 0                # Incremented each agent turn; helps detect runaway sessions
  blocking_reason: ""          # Only populated when status == "blocked"; explains why
```

---

## 2. `decisions_made`

A log of architectural or implementation decisions with full rationale.
Append a new entry; never delete entries.

```yaml
decisions_made:
  - id: ""                     # Short unique slug, e.g. "dec-001-java-scoring-approach"
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601 timestamp
    component: ""              # Which key_component or module this decision affects
    languages_affected: []     # Subset of [java, python, go, typescript] impacted by the decision
    decision: ""               # One sentence: what was decided
    rationale: ""              # Why — reference domain concepts (Strike, Spare, Frame, etc.),
                               # framework constraints (Spring Boot, FastAPI, Express), or
                               # pitfalls (e.g. 10th-frame 3-roll edge case, rolls array sized to 21)
    alternatives_considered: []  # List of approaches that were rejected and why
    trade_offs: ""             # Any known downsides of the chosen approach
    references: []             # File paths or line numbers that implement this decision

  # Example (do not keep in live session — illustrative only):
  # - id: "dec-001-leaderboard-in-memory"
  #   timestamp: "2025-01-15T10:35:00Z"
  #   component: "LeaderboardService"
  #   languages_affected: [java]
  #   decision: "Keep leaderboard storage in-memory using a synchronized TreeSet."
  #   rationale: >
  #     The kata scope does not require persistence. An in-memory ConcurrentSkipListSet
  #     sorted by PlayerScore.compareTo() (highest-first) satisfies the GET /api/leaderboard
  #     response without introducing a database dependency.
  #   alternatives_considered:
  #     - "Redis via Spring Data — rejected: adds operational complexity for a kata"
  #     - "H2 in-memory DB — rejected: ORM overhead not justified at this scope"
  #   trade_offs: "All scores lost on JVM restart; unexpected in Docker Compose multi-run scenarios."
  #   references:
  #     - "src/main/java/bowling/service/LeaderboardService.java"
  #     - "src/main/java/bowling/domain/PlayerScore.java"
```

---

## 3. `files_modified`

Tracks every file touched in this session, the reason, and its post-edit state.

```yaml
files_modified:
  - path: ""                   # Relative path from repo root, e.g. "src/main/java/bowling/Bowling.java"
    language: ""               # java | python | go | typescript | yaml | markdown
    component: ""              # Key component name from key_components (or "new" if adding a file)
    change_type: ""            # create | modify | delete | refactor
    reason: ""                 # Why this file was changed — tie back to task_id
    summary_of_changes: ""     # What specifically changed (method added, bug fixed, field renamed, etc.)
    scoring_logic_changed: false  # Boolean — TRUE triggers requirement to sync all four
                                  # implementations: Bowling.java, api/main.py, game.go, index.ts
    tests_added_or_updated: false # Boolean — did corresponding test file change too?
    corresponding_test_path: ""   # Path to the test file for this source file, e.g.:
                                  #   java  → src/test/java/bowling/BowlingTest.java
                                  #   python → api/test_scoring.py
                                  #   go    → pkg/bowling/game_test.go
                                  #   ts    → services/scoring-service/src/scoring.test.ts
    build_impact: ""           # none | recompile | restart-service | rebuild-docker
                               # "restart-service" if Spring Boot / FastAPI / Express must restart
                               # "rebuild-docker" only if docker-compose.yml is affected (forbidden)
    restricted: false          # TRUE if this path appears in restricted_write_paths — agent should
                               # never set this to true and proceed; it must stop and flag to human
    diff_summary: ""           # One-paragraph plain-English description of the diff
```

---

## 4. `tests_run`

Records every test execution during the session with outcomes and coverage notes.

```yaml
tests_run:
  - run_id: ""                 # Unique slug, e.g. "run-001-java-unit"
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601 timestamp
    language: ""               # java | python | go | typescript
    framework: ""              # junit5 | pytest | jest | go-test
    command: ""                # Exact command executed, e.g.:
                               #   java:       "mvn test"
                               #   python:     "pytest api/" (must run from repo root per pitfall)
                               #   go:         "go test ./pkg/bowling/..."
                               #   typescript: "npm test" (run inside services/scoring-service/)
    scope: ""                  # unit | integration | all
    test_files_targeted: []    # Specific test files run, e.g.:
                               #   - "src/test/java/bowling/BowlingTest.java"
                               #   - "src/test/java/bowling/GameControllerTest.java"
                               #   - "api/test_scoring.py"
                               #   - "pkg/bowling/game_test.go"
                               #   - "services/scoring-service/src/scoring.test.ts"
    result: ""                 # passed | failed | errored | partial
    passed: 0                  # Count of passing tests
    failed: 0                  # Count of failing tests
    errored: 0                 # Count of tests that threw unexpected errors (not assertion failures)
    skipped: 0                 # Count of skipped tests
    failure_details: []        # List of { test_name, error_message, file, line } for each failure
    tenth_frame_coverage: false # Boolean — does the run include the 10th-frame 3-roll edge cases?
                                # This is the highest-risk area per potential_pitfalls.
    perfect_game_coverage: false # Boolean — does the run cover a 12-strike perfect game (score=300)?
    notes: ""                  # Any relevant observations, e.g. port conflict in TS test environment,
                               # pytest path issue (must import from 'api.main', not relative)
```

---

## 5. `open_questions`

Items that need human review, clarification, or a decision the agent cannot make alone.

```yaml
open_questions:
  - id: ""                     # Short slug, e.g. "oq-001-java-version-mismatch"
    timestamp: ""              # ISO-8601 timestamp when the question was raised
    priority: ""               # high | medium | low
    category: ""               # architecture | bug | policy | dependency | environment | scoring-sync
                               # "scoring-sync" = question about keeping four implementations aligned
    question: ""               # The specific question or ambiguity
    context: ""                # Why this came up — reference files, domain concepts, or pitfalls
    affects_languages: []      # Subset of [java, python, go, typescript] affected
    affects_components: []     # Key component names affected
    blocking: false            # TRUE if the agent cannot proceed without an answer
    resolved: false            # Set to TRUE once a human or the agent has answered
    resolution: ""             # Filled in when resolved — what was decided and by whom
    resolved_at: ""            # ISO-8601 timestamp of resolution

  # Standing questions pre-populated from known pitfalls:
  - id: "oq-standing-001-java-version"
    timestamp: "session-start"
    priority: high
    category: environment
    question: >
      pom.xml targets JDK 21 but CI uses JDK 17. Which JDK version should be used
      for local development and agent-driven builds?
    context: >
      Tests may pass in CI (JDK 17) but fail locally if JDK 21 features are used.
      Agents should flag any use of JDK 21-specific APIs.
    affects_languages: [java]
    affects_components: ["Bowling", "BowlingApplication", "GameController", "LeaderboardService"]
    blocking: false
    resolved: false
    resolution: ""
    resolved_at: ""

  - id: "oq-standing-002-scoring-sync"
    timestamp: "session-start"
    priority: high
    category: scoring-sync
    question: >
      When bowling scoring logic is changed in one implementation, what is the
      canonical reference — Java, Python, Go, or TypeScript — for verifying
      all four are in sync?
    context: >
      The four implementations (Bowling.java, api/main.py, pkg/bowling/game.go,
      services/scoring-service/src/index.ts) must produce identical scores for
      identical roll sequences. There is no cross-language integration test.
    affects_languages: [java, python, go, typescript]
    affects_components: ["Bowling", "FastAPI main", "Go bowling game", "scoring-service index"]
    blocking: false
    resolved: false
    resolution: ""
    resolved_at: ""

  - id: "oq-standing-003-leaderboard-persistence"
    timestamp: "session-start"
    priority: low
    category: architecture
    question: >
      The LeaderboardService is in-memory only. Should scores be persisted to
      Redis (already in docker-compose.yml) between restarts?
    context: >
      Current implementation loses all scores on JVM restart. Redis is already
      orchestrated in docker-compose.yml but not connected to LeaderboardService.
    affects_languages: [java]
    affects_components: ["LeaderboardService", "PlayerScore"]
    blocking: false
    resolved: false
    resolution: ""
    resolved_at: ""
```

## 6. `domain_state`

Tracks
